articles.title: New treatment cuts bad cholesterol by nearly 50% without statins
articles.description: A new breakthrough could change how high cholesterol is treated, offering a powerful alternative to traditional drugs. Researchers have developed tiny DNA-based molecules that shut down PCSK9—a key protein that keeps “bad” LDL cholesterol circulating in the blood. By blocking this protein, cells can absorb more cholesterol instead of letting it build up in arteries, dramatically lowering levels linked to heart disease.

articles.title: BAE faces £120m lawsuit over decision to scrap support for aid aircraft
articles.description:
EnComm Aviation says the firm’s action has cut off vital support for crisis-hit countries including South Sudan and the DRC
Britain’s biggest weapons manufacturer, BAE Systems, is facing a £120m lawsuit after scrapping support for aircraft used to deliver aid to some of the world’s neediest countries.
EnComm Aviation, a Kenya-based aid cargo operator, claims the decision forced the cancellation of humanitarian contracts and reduced supplies to South Sudan, now threatened by famine, Somalia and the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC), among others.
 Continue reading...

articles.title: Iran war may cause food shortages in Africa, world’s largest fertiliser firm says
articles.description:
Yara CEO warns of global auction that would leave poorest countries scrambling for supplies they can ill afford
The Iran war could have “dramatic consequences”, causing food shortages and price rises in some of Africa’s poorest and most vulnerable communities, the head of the world’s largest fertiliser company has said.
Svein Tore Holsether, the chief executive of Yara International, said world leaders needed to guard against soaring prices and shortages of fertiliser causing a de facto global auction that would leave the poorest countries, particularly in Africa, scrambling for supplies they could ill afford.
 Continue reading...

articles.description: This week, the federal government's been busy. There are paint jobs, fresh indictments, commemorative items and more. If you've been paying attention — good job!

articles.title: How to talk to boys about looksmaxxing
articles.description: The trend among boys and young men of optimizing their physical appearance includes dangerous practices. Experts offer advice on how to talk to their sons about body image and healthy behaviors.

articles.title: ACTING LABOR SECRETARY SONDERLING: A fast-track way to get a job without college debt
articles.description: President Trump's Department of Labor promotes apprenticeships as alternatives to college, with those who complete earning $86,000 average starting salaries.

articles.title: Dana White 'wasn't going to see 65' before longevity expert intervened with strict new regimen
articles.description: Human biologist Gary Brecka says alarming blood work showed UFC CEO Dana White had only about 10 years left to live without major lifestyle changes.

articles.title: Himanta's 'pushing back Bangladeshis in night' remark stirs row with Dhaka
articles.description: Himantas pushing back Bangladeshis in night remark stirs row with Dhaka

articles.title: Vance, Cruz, head to Iowa on 2026 missions as 2028 GOP race to succeed Trump heats up
articles.description: Vice President JD Vance and Sen. Ted Cruz make stops in Iowa this week, fueling 2028 presidential speculation ahead of the 2026 midterm elections.

articles.title: Janet Mills stood up to Trump. Democratic voters shrugged.
articles.description: Liberals say they want a fighter. But a popular governor who clashed with the president failed to catch fire in Maine’s Senate race.

topStories.title: Could the FCC really yank ABC's TV licenses amid Trump spat with Kimmel?
topStories.description: Federal telecom regulators can revoke broadcast licenses, but legal experts say the FCC would face a tough road in forcing ABC to go dark.

topStories.title: Delays in visa program threaten placement of hundreds of doctors in underserved areas
topStories.description: Hundreds of foreign doctors about to complete training in the U.S. will have to leave the country if the federal government doesn't rapidly process their visa waiver applications, immigration attorneys say.

topStories.title: Meet the horses running in the Kentucky Derby
topStories.description: The Kentucky Derby will see a full field of 20 horses in the first leg of the 2026 competition for horse racing's Triple Crown.

topStories.title: Nationwide May Day protests expected to pick up mantle of 'No Kings'
topStories.description: The protest organizers are calling for a boycott of work, school and shopping to protest Trump administration policies and what activists describe as a billionaire takeover of government.

topStories.title: It's Day 1 of Medicaid work requirements in Nebraska. People are worried
topStories.description: Starting May 1, many people covered by Medicaid in Nebraska have to prove they are working. It's a requirement most states will have to implement under President Trump's budget law, beginning in January.

dailyNews.title: Passenger railroads see a sharp jump in ridership as gas prices climb
dailyNews.description: More Americans are turning to the train as gas prices reach their highest point since the war in Iran began. Brightline, the privately-run railroad in Florida, had its best month ever in March.

dailyNews.title: A key Senate rule is slowly losing its grip, and both parties know it
dailyNews.description: Senate Majority Leader John Thune has resisted President Donald Trump’s pleas to kill the filibuster, but Republicans circumvented it to try to fund immigration enforcement agencies.

dailyNews.title: From 3 million barrels a day to potential shutdown: How the US blockade is strangling Iran's oil sector
dailyNews.description: Iran's oil sector faces a crisis due to a US naval blockade, limiting exports and storage. Experts warn that halted production could lead to job losses and long-term damage to oil wells, straining an already fragile economy and causing rising gasoline prices globally.

dailyNews.title: US Economy Is in 'Fine Shape,' Marathon's Richards Says
dailyNews.description: Bruce Richards, Marathon Asset Management CEO, co-founder and chairman, says the US economy is "in good shape." Speaking on Bloomberg Television, he adds that consumer consumption looks "rock solid." Richards also discusses the impact prolonged elevated oil prices could have on the economic situation and growth.  (Source: Bloomberg)

dailyNews.title: Supreme Court reserves verdict on Pawan Khera’s plea for pre-arrest bail in Assam case
dailyNews.description: The case is ‘unprecedented’ with Chief Minister Himanta Biswa Sarma himself making statements against the Congress leader, senior advocate Abhishek Manu Singhvi tells the Supreme Court

dailyNews.title: Opinion: America is worrying about fertility again. But it’s not really about families
dailyNews.description: Where the state once worried about too many births, it now worries about too few.

dailyNews.title: Iran war pushes oil prices to 4-year high as Hegseth faces lawmakers
dailyNews.description: Oil prices hit a 4-year high as Axios reports Trump will hear new options to try to break the Strait of Hormuz standoff with Iran with a new wave of attacks.

dailyNews.title: Iran vows ‘long, painful’ response if US renews attacks
dailyNews.description: Diplomatic efforts to resolve the conflict have hit an impasse, with a ceasefire in place since April 8.

dailyNews.title: Remarks on Himanta Sarma's wife: SC grants anticipatory bail to Pawan Khera in alleged defamation case
dailyNews.description: The Supreme Court granted anticipatory bail to Congress leader Pawan Khera in a defamation and forgery case involving Assam CM Himanta Biswa Sarma's wife.

dailyNews.title: U.K. raises terrorism threat level to "severe" after stabbing attack
dailyNews.description: The U.K. has raised its national threat level from "substantial" to "severe," citing the increasing threat of Islamist and extreme right-wing terrorism in the country.

dailyNews.title: Suspect arrested for allegedly running meth lab at Michigan State University’s largest academic building
dailyNews.description: A suspect is facing felony charges after allegedly running a secret meth lab operation at Michigan State University's Wells Hall, police said.

dailyNews.title: 5 wounded in possible stabbing attack at Washington high school
dailyNews.description: The suspect, a student, was detained at the scene, police said.

topStories.description: Under the 1973 War Powers Resolution, a U.S. president needs to withdraw troops 60 days after reporting their deployment to Congress

dailyNews.title: Video shows Cole Allen casing hotel, storming checkpoint in alleged Trump assassination attempt
dailyNews.description: U.S. Attorney Jeanine Pirro said there is no evidence that a Secret Service shot during the incident was the victim of friendly fire.

dailyNews.title: New video shows WHCD suspect inside hotel before rushing security checkpoint with weapon
dailyNews.description: Newly released Justice Department footage appears to show the White House Correspondents' Dinner shooting suspect rushing a security checkpoint armed.

dailyNews.title: Trump’s Plans to Boost Weapons Production Might Not Deliver for Years
dailyNews.description: While the defense industry has announced plans to make more munitions, much of that expanded production will not quickly kick in.

dailyNews.title: Comey Indictment Shows Justice Dept. Got the Message From Bondi’s Firing
dailyNews.description: In naming only an interim successor as acting attorney general, President Trump has established even greater incentives to execute his most extreme demands, current and former officials say.

dailyNews.title: Decoding the King: Brits Hear Subtle Rebuke to Trump that Americans Might Miss
dailyNews.description: Royal watchers in Britain called the visit of King Charles III to America a master class in understated criticism.

dailyNews.title: Last on King Charles’s U.S. Tour: A Potluck and a Win for Scottish Whisky
dailyNews.description: King Charles III and Queen Camilla had a ceremonial farewell at the White House before attending a block party in Front Royal, Va.

dailyNews.title: As Iran war nears key 60-day deadline, Congress and Trump face choices
dailyNews.description: The War Powers Resolution sets deadlines for the president to end hostilities without congressional approval.

dailyNews.title: New White House drug abuse strategy floats wastewater testing, AI, more treatment
dailyNews.description: The Trump administration is proposing wastewater testing to try to ferret out data on illegal drug use in real time, according to a draft of a new drug control strategy obtained by CBS News. It also proposes using AI to track threats.

dailyNews.title: Breaking down OPEC and its influence as UAE decides to leave
dailyNews.description: President Trump gave his endorsement to the United Arab Emirates' decision to leave the Organization of the Petroleum Exporting Countries, or OPEC, on Wednesday. "The Daily Report" breaks it down.

dailyNews.title: CBS News' Fin Gómez reflects on correspondents' dinner shooting: "Incredible force of reporters"
dailyNews.description: CBS News political director and executive director of politics and White House Fin Gómez reflects on the White House Correspondents' Dinner attack on "The Daily Report," saying he was proud of "our fellow colleagues."

dailyNews.title: "It's amazing how resilient consumer spending has been" amid the war, analyst says
dailyNews.description: The national average gas price is up to $4.30 per gallon, the highest in four years, as President Trump said Thursday that gas prices "will drop like a rock" when the Iran war ends. Ted Rossman, principal analyst at Bankrate, joins "The Daily Report" with more.

dailyNews.title: Confrontation between officers and alleged shooter at D.C. gala lasted 7 seconds
dailyNews.description: Seven seconds passed between when the alleged gunman at Saturday's White House Correspondents' Dinner — carrying a shotgun initially concealed by a jacket — first encountered federal law enforcement and when he was subdued, sources told CBS News.
